name: LabWindows/CVI Callback, UI Event and Run-Time Engine Catalog
notes: The standard callback typedefs are shared by CVI 2012 and CVI 2020.
EVENT_RING_BEGIN_MENU, EVENT_TAB_PAGE_CLOSE and EVENT_MOUSE_WHEEL_CLICK are present in the CVI 2020 reference header and absent from the CVI 2012 header.
Event numbers 1000 through 10000 are reserved for user programs according to userint.h.
Version 1.1.0 groups EVENT_* macros into family selectors so the explorer is compact while numeric values remain visible in the picker.
Callback pointer typedef cards are declarative parameterized templates rather than static historical snippets.
The cvirte.h lifecycle API is exposed through InitCVIRTE, InitCVIRTEEx, CVIRTEHasBeenDetached and CloseCVIRTE cards.
Version 1.2.0 consolidates the former CVI Patterns & References library into CVI Basics through dedicated Keywords and Examples / Workflows categories.

callbackTypes.name: PanelCallbackPtr
callbackTypes.declaration: typedef int (CVICALLBACK * PanelCallbackPtr)(int panel, int event, void *callbackData, int eventData1, int eventData2);
callbackTypes.description: Panel callback invoked for panel-level events.

callbackTypes.name: CtrlCallbackPtr
callbackTypes.declaration: typedef int (CVICALLBACK * CtrlCallbackPtr)(int panel, int control, int event, void *callbackData, int eventData1, int eventData2);
callbackTypes.description: Control callback invoked for control-level events.

callbackTypes.name: MenuCallbackPtr
callbackTypes.declaration: typedef void (CVICALLBACK * MenuCallbackPtr)(int menubar, int menuItem, void *callbackData, int panel);
callbackTypes.description: Menu or menu-item callback invoked when the item is selected.

callbackTypes.name: MenuDimmerCallbackPtr
callbackTypes.declaration: typedef void (CVICALLBACK * MenuDimmerCallbackPtr)(int menuBar, int panel);
callbackTypes.description: Menu dimmer callback used to update enabled or dimmed menu states.

callbackTypes.name: MainCallbackPtr
callbackTypes.declaration: typedef int (CVICALLBACK * MainCallbackPtr)(int panelOrMenuBar, int controlOrMenuItem, int event, void *callbackData, int eventData1, int eventData2);
callbackTypes.description: Global callback for main UI events including EVENT_END_TASK and optional idle events.

callbackTypes.name: DeferredCallbackPtr
callbackTypes.description: Deferred callback dispatched with PostDeferredCall or PostDeferredCallToThread.

callbackTypes.name: CellCompareCallbackPtr
callbackTypes.declaration: typedef int (CVICALLBACK * CellCompareCallbackPtr)(int panel, int control, Point item1, Point item2, void *callbackData);
callbackTypes.description: Comparison callback used when sorting table cells.

callbackTypes.name: TreeCellCompareCallbackPtr
callbackTypes.declaration: typedef int (CVICALLBACK * TreeCellCompareCallbackPtr)(int panel, int control, int item1, int item2, int keyCol, void *callbackData);
callbackTypes.description: Comparison callback used when sorting tree cells.

callbackTypes.name: CtrlMenuCallbackPtr
callbackTypes.declaration: typedef void (CVICALLBACK * CtrlMenuCallbackPtr)(int panel, int control, int menuItem, void *callbackData);

callbackTypes.name: WinMsgCallbackPtr
callbackTypes.declaration: typedef void (CVICALLBACK * WinMsgCallbackPtr)(WinMsgWParam wParam, WinMsgLParam lParam, void *callbackData);

events.name: EVENT_NONE
events.description: No event. Useful as a neutral value when initializing or documenting an event variable.

events.name: EVENT_COMMIT
events.description: Commit or activation event. Common for buttons, menu selections, tables and trees. For tables, eventData1/eventData2 identify the affected row and column.

events.name: EVENT_VAL_CHANGED
events.description: Control value changed. For tables, eventData1/eventData2 identify the affected cell. For trees, eventData1 describes the tree sub-event.

events.name: EVENT_LEFT_CLICK
events.description: Left mouse-button press. eventData1 is the vertical position and eventData2 is the horizontal position.

events.name: EVENT_LEFT_DOUBLE_CLICK
events.description: Left mouse-button double-click. eventData1 is the vertical position and eventData2 is the horizontal position.

events.name: EVENT_RIGHT_CLICK
events.description: Right mouse-button press. eventData1 is the vertical position and eventData2 is the horizontal position.

events.name: EVENT_RIGHT_DOUBLE_CLICK
events.description: Right mouse-button double-click. eventData1 is the vertical position and eventData2 is the horizontal position.

events.name: EVENT_KEYPRESS
events.description: Keyboard event. Use the KeyPressEvent helper functions to decode or modify eventData2.

events.name: EVENT_GOT_FOCUS
events.description: Focus gained. eventData1 contains the previous control with focus.

events.name: EVENT_LOST_FOCUS
events.description: Focus lost. eventData1 contains the new control with focus.

events.name: EVENT_IDLE
events.description: Idle callback notification. InstallMainCallback can request idle events.

events.name: EVENT_CLOSE
events.description: Panel close request. A panel callback typically handles this event to call QuitUserInterface or hide the panel.

events.name: EVENT_PANEL_SIZE
events.description: Panel size changed after resizing.

events.name: EVENT_PANEL_MOVE
events.description: Panel position changed after moving.

events.name: EVENT_END_TASK
events.description: Application termination request sent to the main callback. Return 0 to quit or 1 to abort termination. eventData1 distinguishes APP_CLOSE and SYSTEM_CLOSE.

events.name: EVENT_TIMER_TICK
events.description: Timer tick. Use GetTimerTickData to retrieve the current time and elapsed interval portably.

events.name: EVENT_DISCARD
events.description: Panel or control discarded.

events.name: EVENT_EXPAND
events.description: Expandable item expanded, typically in tree-like controls.

events.name: EVENT_COLLAPSE
events.description: Expandable item collapsed, typically in tree-like controls.

events.name: EVENT_DRAG
events.description: Drag operation in progress.

events.name: EVENT_DROP
events.description: Drop operation requested.

events.name: EVENT_DROPPED
events.description: Drop operation completed.

events.name: EVENT_SORT
events.description: Sort request.

events.name: EVENT_SELECTION_CHANGE
events.description: Selection changed.

events.name: EVENT_HSCROLL
events.description: Horizontal scroll event.

events.name: EVENT_VSCROLL
events.description: Vertical scroll event.

events.name: EVENT_MARK_STATE_CHANGE
events.description: Mark-state change in controls such as tree controls.

events.name: EVENT_COMBO_BOX_INSERT
events.description: Combo-box insertion. eventData1/eventData2 identify the related table cell when applicable.

events.name: EVENT_ACTIVE_CELL_CHANGE
events.description: Active table cell changed.

events.name: EVENT_ROW_SIZE_CHANGE
events.description: Table row size changed. eventData1 contains the row.

events.name: EVENT_COLUMN_SIZE_CHANGE
events.description: Table column size changed. eventData2 contains the column.

events.name: EVENT_ACTIVE_TAB_CHANGE
events.description: Active tab changed. eventData1 is the old page and eventData2 is the new page.

events.name: EVENT_EDIT_MODE_STATE_CHANGE
events.description: Edit-mode state changed. eventData1 is the new edit state and eventData2 is the index.

events.name: EVENT_MOUSE_POINTER_MOVE
events.description: Mouse pointer moved. eventData1/eventData2 contain vertical/horizontal coordinates.

events.name: EVENT_LEFT_CLICK_UP
events.description: Left mouse-button release. eventData1/eventData2 contain vertical/horizontal coordinates.

events.name: EVENT_RIGHT_CLICK_UP
events.description: Right mouse-button release. eventData1/eventData2 contain vertical/horizontal coordinates.

events.name: EVENT_MOUSE_WHEEL_SCROLL
events.description: Mouse-wheel scroll. eventData1 is a MOUSE_WHEEL_* action and eventData2 is the amount to scroll.

events.name: EVENT_PANEL_SIZING
events.description: Panel is being resized. eventData1 is a PANEL_SIZING_* edge; use GetPanelEventRect or SetPanelEventRect with eventData2.

events.name: EVENT_PANEL_MOVING
events.description: Panel is being moved. Use GetPanelEventRect or SetPanelEventRect with eventData2.

events.name: EVENT_RADIX_CHANGE
events.description: Numeric radix or format changed. eventData1 is the new format.

events.name: EVENT_ZOOM
events.description: Graph zoom action. eventData1 is a ZOOM_* value.

events.name: EVENT_AXIS_VAL_CHANGE
events.description: Graph axis value changed. eventData1 identifies the axis.

events.name: EVENT_INTERACTIVE_LEGEND
events.description: Interactive graph legend action. eventData1 is the plot identifier and eventData2 is the built-in control-menu identifier.

events.name: EVENT_TABLE_ROW_COL_LABEL_CLICK
events.description: Table row or column label clicked. eventData1 is the row and eventData2 is the column.

events.name: EVENT_BEGIN_EDIT_TREE_CELL
events.description: Tree-cell edit is about to begin. eventData1 is the row and eventData2 is the column.

events.name: EVENT_TREE_CELL_COMMIT
events.description: Tree-cell edit committed. eventData1 is the row and eventData2 is the column.

events.name: EVENT_TREE_CELL_BEGIN_MENU
events.description: Tree-cell contextual menu is about to open.

events.name: EVENT_TREE_CELL_ACTIVE_ITEM_CHANGE
events.description: Tree-cell active item changed. eventData1 is the row and eventData2 is the column.

events.name: EVENT_TREE_CELL_VAL_CHANGED
events.description: Tree-cell value changed. eventData1 is the row and eventData2 is the column.

events.name: EVENT_CTRL_MENU
events.description: Built-in or custom control menu action.

events.name: EVENT_DROP_COPY
events.description: Drop-copy operation.

events.name: EVENT_VAL_COERCED
events.description: Table value coerced. eventData1/eventData2 identify the affected cell.

events.name: EVENT_PANEL_MINIMIZE
events.description: Panel minimized.

events.name: EVENT_PANEL_MAXIMIZE
events.description: Panel maximized.

events.name: EVENT_PANEL_RESTORE
events.description: Panel restored from minimized or maximized state.

events.name: EVENT_RING_BEGIN_MENU
events.description: Ring control menu is about to open. Present in the CVI 2020 userint.h reference header.

events.name: EVENT_TAB_PAGE_CLOSE
events.description: Tab page close requested. eventData1 is the page index and eventData2 is 0. Present in the CVI 2020 userint.h reference header.

events.name: EVENT_MOUSE_WHEEL_CLICK
events.description: Mouse-wheel button click. eventData1/eventData2 contain vertical/horizontal coordinates. Present in the CVI 2020 userint.h reference header.

eventSpecifiers.name: MARK_STATE_CHANGE
eventSpecifiers.description: Tree callback sub-event: mark state changed.

eventSpecifiers.name: LABEL_CHANGE
eventSpecifiers.description: Tree callback sub-event: item label changed.

eventSpecifiers.name: ACTIVE_ITEM_CHANGE
eventSpecifiers.description: Tree callback sub-event: active item changed.

eventSpecifiers.name: COLUMN_LABEL_CLICK
eventSpecifiers.description: Tree callback sub-event: column label clicked.

eventSpecifiers.name: SYSTEM_CLOSE
eventSpecifiers.description: EVENT_END_TASK origin: operating-system shutdown or close.

runtimeLifecycle.name: InitCVIRTE
runtimeLifecycle.signature: int CVIFUNC InitCVIRTE(void *hInstance, char *argv[], void *reserved); /* macro alias of InitCVIRTEEx */
runtimeLifecycle.description: Initialize the LabWindows/CVI Run-Time Engine through the documented InitCVIRTE macro alias.

runtimeLifecycle.name: InitCVIRTEEx
runtimeLifecycle.description: Initialize the LabWindows/CVI Run-Time Engine explicitly. InitCVIRTE is a macro alias for this function.

runtimeLifecycle.name: CVIRTEHasBeenDetached
runtimeLifecycle.description: Report whether the CVI Run-Time Engine has already been detached.

runtimeLifecycle.name: CloseCVIRTE
runtimeLifecycle.description: Close the LabWindows/CVI Run-Time Engine after the application no longer requires it.

runtimeLifecycle.name: Executable CVI Run-Time Engine initialization recipe
runtimeLifecycle.description: Minimal executable-side initialization and shutdown sequence for code that calls CVI runtime APIs.

runtimeLifecycle.name: DLL-side CVI Run-Time Engine initialization recipe
runtimeLifecycle.description: Minimal DLL-side Run-Time Engine initialization sequence. Keep heavy work out of DllMain whenever possible.

keywords.name: CVICALLBACK
keywords.description: CVI callback calling-convention marker used by panel, control, timer and asynchronous callbacks.

keywords.name: CVIFUNC
keywords.description: CVI exported-function calling-convention marker used by the LabWindows/CVI runtime headers.

examplesWorkflows.name: CVI control callback skeleton
examplesWorkflows.description: Minimal static callback example retained as a compact reference workflow.

examplesWorkflows.name: Load, display and discard a panel
SetCtrlVal and GetCtrlVal
examplesWorkflows.description: Read-modify-write workflow for a numeric control.

examplesWorkflows.name: CVI callbackData context
examplesWorkflows.description: Typed callbackData context workflow avoiding global state.
